The most recently created open issue is "Support multi-organization membership and org switching" (ENG-277), created on 2026-02-19. 

Here are the details:
- **Title:** Support multi-organization membership and org switching
- **Identifier:** ENG-277
- **Status:** Backlog
- **Created At:** 2026-02-19T22:11:10.000Z
- **Description:** Our current organization model is effectively single-org-per-user and causes lock-in.

Today:

* New users are auto-assigned a tenant on first authenticated request.
* A user can only belong to one tenant at a time (`users.tenantId`).
* Accepting an invite to another org is blocked (`User belongs to a different tenant`).
* There is no org-switching UX.

This issue refines the architecture to support **multi-organization membership + … (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

task_result.final_answer:
Of course. Here are the details of the most recently created open issue:

**Issue: ENG-277 - Support multi-organization membership and org switching**

*   **Identifier**: ENG-277
*   **Status**: Backlog
*   **Team**: Kontext
*   **Created**: February 19, 2026
*   **URL**: [https://linear.app/cobrowser/issue/ENG-277/support-multi-organization-membership-and-org-switching](https://linear.app/cobrowser/issue/ENG-277/support-multi-organization-membership-and-org-switching)
*   **Linked Attachment**: [GitHub Issue #249](https://github.com/kontext-dev/spaces/issues/249)

**Summary:**
The current system architecture only allows a user to belong to a single organization, which causes lock-in and prevents standard collaboration flows. This issue proposes a significant refactor to support multi-organization membership and allow users to easily switch between different organizations, similar to the user experience on platforms like GitHub or Clerk. The issue outlines a detailed, multi-phase migration plan to achieve this, including data model changes, API updates, and frontend enhancements.
